dependency_notes: Stories are implemented sequentially (per commands/feature.md non-goal on parallel story implementation), so no story mocks a later story's output — S2 onward call the real prior-story functions directly. Ordering follows the codec-before-consumers rule: S1 (pure codec: encode/decode/parse/sign/verify) is the foundation every later story imports. S2 (cache-import adapter) must land before any UI story writes to contactCache via a card. S3 (profile nickname cap) has no hard dependency on S1/S2 but is sequenced third as a small, independent, low-complexity increment before the heavier UI wiring. S4 (Add Contact wiring) builds the shared card-aware QR-scan seam (qr.ts / NpubQrScanner / NpubQrModal onScan contract) as its first consumer, which S5 (group invite) then reuses without rebuilding it — this is why S5 follows S4 rather than running in parallel. S6 (share action) only needs S1's encode/share-URL seam and could technically land earlier, but is sequenced after the scan-side stories since it touches the same NpubQrModal.tsx file in its 'display' mode and benefits from that file having stabilized. S7 (/add onboarding page) is last because it re-uses the add-contact flow (S4) and the cache-import seam (S2) end-to-end, and owns the final cross-epic i18n completeness check (AC-INTL-1) once all UI copy has been added by S3-S7.
mock_resolution_plan: No temporary mocks introduced. Sequential implementation means each story consumes the real prior-story artifact (parseContactCard, importCard, encodeCard) rather than a stand-in; mocks-registry.json remains empty.

invariants: Given a bare valid npub, returns { pubkeyHex } with no profile field (AC-PARSE-1).

Given a card link (…/add#c=<b64url>) or a raw base64url card payload with a valid signed card, returns { pubkeyHex, profile: { nickname, updatedAt } } where updatedAt is an ISO-8601 string derived from created_at (AC-PARSE-2, AC-PARSE-3).
Given a signed card whose signature does not verify, returns { error } and never a usable pubkeyHex+profile pair — no silent downgrade to a bare-pubkey add (AC-PARSE-4).
Given input that is neither a valid npub nor a decodable card, returns { error } and never throws (AC-PARSE-5).
Same contract as the S1→S2 seam; AddContactModal must treat an error result as add-failure, not as a bare-pubkey fallback (AC-PARSE-4).
InviteMemberModal uses only pubkeyHex from the result and MUST discard profile — group-invite-by-card never writes contactCache (DD 8, AC-GRP-1).
pages/add.tsx reads window.location.hash, extracts #c=, and calls parseContactCard exactly once per load; the parsed result (not the raw hash) is what survives the onboarding step (AC-UX-7).
Upserts contactCache via writeContactEntry with nickname = card name and updatedAt = new Date(created_at * 1000).toISOString() (AC-CACHE-1).
LWW holds across sources: a card whose created_at predates an existing ISO updatedAt entry (e.g. from MLS profile sync) does not overwrite it (AC-CACHE-2).
Re-importing the same card twice is idempotent — the second import leaves contactCache unchanged (AC-CACHE-3).
Importing a name-only card for a pubkey with an existing cached avatar preserves that avatar; only nickname is updated (AC-CACHE-4).
Same contract as the S2→S4 seam; the onboarding-then-add path calls importCard only after a local identity exists (AC-UX-7).

destination.artifact: NpubQrModal display mode / settings.tsx share action

fields.name: payload
fields.type: string (base64url card bytes, RFC 4648 §5, no padding)

invariants: encodeCard output round-trips through decodeCard to the same pubkeyHex and name (AC-CARD-1).
A signed payload at the 32-UTF-8-byte name cap is ≤ 180 base64url characters, and shareUrl is ≤ 205 characters (AC-CARD-4).
The signature verifies via verifyEvent for a freshly encoded card in local, NIP-07, and NIP-46 signer modes (AC-SIG-1, AC-UX-4).
When the user has no nickname set, encodeCard produces an unsigned (SIGNED-unset) card equivalent to a bare npub (AC-CARD-6, DD 10).

stories.name: contact-card-codec
stories.title: Contact card binary codec: encode, decode, parse, sign/verify
stories.description: New pure module app/src/lib/contactCard.ts implementing the v1 binary contact-card format: header (version+flags), pubkey, created_at, name_len, name, sig. Provides encodeCard (sign via the active EventSigner, reusing the canonical CARD_CONTENT builder), decodeCard, and parseContactCard (discriminates bare npub / card URL fragment / raw base64url payload). Verification reconstructs the NIP-01 kind-0 event, attaches a computed id via getEventHash, and calls verifyEvent. No React, no storage, no relay calls — matches the project's pure-function testing discipline (no jsdom).

includes: base64url encode/decode helper (RFC 4648 §5, no padding) — none exists in the repo, must add
v1 byte layout: header (1) + pubkey (32) + created_at (4, uint32 BE) + name_len (1) + name (name_len) + sig (64), present iff SIGNED
strict v1 header rejection: version bits > 0, HAS_AVATAR bit set, or any reserved flag bit set MUST reject
32-UTF-8-byte name cap enforced at encode with codepoint-boundary truncation (never mid-character)
canonical CARD_CONTENT(name) builder shared by encode and decode, including unpaired-surrogate normalization/rejection at sign time
signature construction/verification as a NIP-01 kind-0 event { pubkey, created_at, kind:0, tags:[], content: CARD_CONTENT(name) }, with id = getEventHash(event) attached before verifyEvent
parseContactCard(input): npub1… -> npubToPubkeyHex, no profile; URL (…/add#c=<b64url>) -> extract fragment then decode; else raw base64url payload
unsigned (pubkey-only) card handling: SIGNED unset decodes to { pubkeyHex } with no profile
excludes: UI wiring at any entry point (owned by S3-S7)
share-URL construction beyond a pure buildShareUrl(payload) helper (share UI is S6)

stories.notes: Reuse hexToBytes/bytesToHex from @/src/lib/nostrKeys and npubToPubkeyHex/pubkeyToNpub from nostrKeys.ts — do not add a 5th hex reimplementation. Crypto primitives (generateSecretKey, getPublicKey, finalizeEvent, getEventHash, verifyEvent) come from nostr-tools/pure, matching the profileSync.ts:17 and signerAdapter.ts:98 precedent. Test file follows the profileSync.test.ts tamper-and-reject pattern (flip a sig hex nibble -> expect reject) plus new cases for the strict-header and surrogate-normalization ACs.

stories.name: contact-card-import
stories.title: Card-to-cache import adapter (LWW, avatar preservation, idempotency)
stories.description: Adapter over the existing contactCache that turns a parsed card profile into a contactCache upsert: importCard(pubkeyHex, profile) computes the ISO-8601 updatedAt from the card's created_at and calls writeContactEntry, preserving any existing avatar. Also the story that asserts the privacy-invariant ACs for the encode/parse/cache path as a whole.

includes: importCard(pubkeyHex, profile) upserting via writeContactEntry with nickname=card name, updatedAt=new Date(created_at*1000).toISOString()
avatar preservation: avatar = readContactEntry(pubkeyHex)?.avatar ?? null
cross-source LWW test: a card whose created_at predates an existing ISO updatedAt entry (e.g. one written by MLS profile sync) MUST NOT overwrite
idempotency test: importing the same card twice leaves contactCache unchanged

privacy-invariant regression test: encode/sign, parse/verify, and cache-import emit zero relay events, and the repository contains no kind-0 metadata publish to public relays (guards against publishIdentityToRelays being reintroduced)

stories.notes: AC-SEC-1's 'caching' clause and AC-SEC-2's repo-wide publish-removal guard are grouped here because this is the story that completes the full encode->parse->cache pipeline and can assert 'no relay event' end-to-end in one integration-style unit test, plus a grep-based regression test for the removed publishIdentityToRelays call. writeContactEntry/readContactEntry are reused unmodified from contactCache.ts (contactCache.ts:33/47) — no code change needed there, per the architecture module map's 'modified: reused' framing.

stories.name: profile-nickname-cap
stories.title: Enforce 32-UTF-8-byte nickname cap at profile save
stories.description: The nickname field on the profile-edit surface (app/pages/profile.tsx) enforces a 32-UTF-8-byte cap at save time (prevented or truncated at input), with a translated limit indication, so newly-saved nicknames never exceed the cap the card format assumes — making the encoder-side truncation in S1 a migration backstop rather than the common path.

includes: byte-based (not character-count) validation of the nickname input against the 32-UTF-8-byte cap at profile save
translated (en/de) limit message shown when the cap would be exceeded
prevent-or-truncate behavior at the input, not a silent post-hoc truncation with no user feedback
excludes: card-encode-side truncation (already covered by AC-CARD-5 in S1)

stories.notes: Independent of the codec — sequenced third as a small, low-complexity increment. The byte-length check can share a helper with S1's UTF-8 byte counting if convenient, but MUST NOT re-derive its own competing cap logic (Module Ownership: don't recreate what another module owns) — import the byte-length utility from contactCard.ts if S1 exposes one, or keep this check self-contained if S1 does not export one.

stories.name: add-contact-card-wiring
stories.title: Add Contact modal: paste and QR-scan a card, populate nickname on add
stories.description: AddContactModal routes both the typed/pasted input and the QR-scanned input through parseContactCard, then addContactByNpub as today plus importCard for the profile. Because the QR-scan path is shared plumbing (NpubQrScanner -> NpubQrModal onScan -> qr.ts normaliseNpubPayload), this story makes that shared scan seam card-aware for the first time; S5 (group invite) then reuses it without rebuilding it.

includes: AddContactModal.handleAdd routes npubInput through parseContactCard before addContactByNpub
on a valid card, addContactByNpub still seeds knownPeers/contacts as today, and importCard populates the nickname so the new contact renders by name
on a bare npub (no card), addContactByNpub succeeds with no nickname (fallback preserved)
already-existing contact + newer card: refresh cached nickname via importCard even when addContactByNpub returns already_exists (no new contact entry required)
qr.ts gains a card-aware parse function (sibling to normaliseNpubPayload, or normaliseNpubPayload extended) so NpubQrScanner accepts a card payload alongside a bare npub
NpubQrScanner / NpubQrModal onScan contract widened to carry the parsed { pubkeyHex, profile? } (or the raw scanned string plus a parse step at the call site) instead of assuming a bare npub string
AddContactModal's onScan handler for the Add Contact scanner behaves identically to the paste path (AC-UX-2)
excludes: the card codec and cache-import logic themselves (S1, S2)

stories.notes: Widening the onScan contract touches NpubQrModal.tsx and NpubQrScanner.tsx, which are shared with InviteMemberModal (S5) and settings.tsx (S6). This story owns that shared widening because it is the first consumer; S5 depends on it landing first (story_order). Boy-scout note from exploration.json: NpubQrScanner.tsx:146 hardcodes an untranslated 'Starting camera...' string — fix in passing since this story already touches the file (CLAUDE.md code-hygiene rule).

stories.name: group-invite-by-card
stories.title: Group invite-by-npub input accepts a card, down-converts to pubkeyHex
stories.description: InviteMemberModal's npub input (paste and QR-scan, reusing the now-card-aware scan seam from S4) accepts a card link/QR/payload, down-converts it to pubkeyHex via parseContactCard, and calls the unchanged inviteByNpub. Per DD 8, this path MUST NOT write to contactCache or create a contacts/knownPeers entry before the invite's MLS commit lands — the invitee's name arrives via MLS profile sync after joining, as today.

includes: InviteMemberModal.handleInvite (and its QR-scan onScan) routes input through parseContactCard, discards any profile field, and passes only pubkeyHex-derived npub/hex to inviteByNpub
no contactCache write and no contacts/knownPeers entry created for the invitee from this path
KeyPackage dependency unchanged: an invitee with no published KeyPackage fails exactly as invite-by-npub does today
excludes: the shared scan-seam widening (already done in S4)
contactCache write semantics (owned by S2, and explicitly not invoked here)

stories.notes: inviteByNpub (MarmotContext.tsx:1223) stays npub/pubkey-only per DD 1/8 — down-conversion happens at this caller, not inside MarmotContext. Deliberately does not call importCard/writeContactEntry (see S2's contract) to avoid the purgeStrangerContacts race described in DD 8.

stories.name: share-contact-card
stories.title: Share contact card: signed card as onboarding-URL link + QR
stories.description: The existing 'Share' / own-npub QR surface (settings.tsx + NpubQrModal display mode) is augmented to build and cache the current user's signed card (via encodeCard using the active signer: local key, NIP-07, or NIP-46) and encode it as the full onboarding URL https://few.chat/add#c=<b64url> at ECC-L, rather than the bare npub at ECC-M. The signed card is cached and only rebuilt when the nickname changes, so NIP-46 signing is not a round trip on every share-modal open. No nickname set -> unsigned card, equivalent to sharing a bare npub (DD 10).

includes: settings.tsx builds the signed card via encodeCard using activeEventSignerOverride.current ?? createPrivateKeySigner(privateKeyHex), caching the result keyed by nickname
NpubQrModal display mode encodes buildShareUrl(payload) (the full onboarding URL) instead of the bare npub, at ECC-L instead of ECC-M
adapter-level unit tests for card production in local, NIP-07 (stubbed sign), and NIP-46 (stubbed sign) modes, each asserting the produced card verifies via real getEventHash/verifyEvent
cache invalidation only on nickname change, not on every share-modal open
excludes: a local-mode e2e assertion of the full share flow (belongs to the epic's e2e step, not this story's unit-test scope)

stories.notes: No existing test stub for NIP-46/07 signer signing — author fresh, shaping the stub as blockUntilReady()->{pubkey}, sign(event)->sigHex string (NDK 3.0.3 returns sig string only), following the closest existing idiom (callSignaling.test.ts:142,336 vi.mock nostr-tools/pure pattern) per exploration.json's gaps_flagged note.

stories.name: add-onboarding-page
stories.title: /add#c=… static onboarding/deep-link page
stories.description: New static top-level page app/pages/add.tsx reads the card from window.location.hash (fragment, not a query param — DD 9), parses it via parseContactCard, and drives two modes: (a) visitor with a local identity -> pre-filled add-contact flow; (b) visitor with no local identity -> onboarding (identity creation) first, holding the card in page state (or the retained hash) across the onboarding step, then completing the add. Also owns the epic-wide i18n completeness check once all new UI copy (S3-S7) exists.

includes: new static page app/pages/add.tsx, SSR-safe (typeof window guards), resolves on direct load/reload
reads window.location.hash, extracts #c=, calls parseContactCard
mode (a): existing local identity -> drives the existing add-contact flow, pre-filled from the card
mode (b): no local identity -> onboarding/identity-creation UI, card held in memory/retained hash, completes the add once identity exists
the card is never transmitted to the server (fragment-only) in either mode

epic-wide i18n completeness test: every user-facing string added across S3-S7 has both en and de entries in app/src/lib/i18n.ts, referenced via useCopy() (REQUIRED_KEYS-loop pattern per feedback.i18n.test.ts)
excludes: the group-join deep link (pages/groups.tsx admin param / JoinRequestCard) — exempt from the one-parser rule, out of scope entirely (spec: 'Related changes' / Technical approach exemption)
the add-contact flow's own logic (reused from S4, not reimplemented here)

stories.notes: hash-based routing (window.location.hash) is unprecedented in this repo (all existing pages use router.query per exploration.json's hash_routing_note) — author fresh with typeof window guards; not a blocker, just no existing pattern to copy. AC-INTL-1 is assigned here (last-landing story) as the single comprehensive i18n-parity check across every string this epic added, rather than being split per-story, since the AC's own wording ('every user-facing string added by this epic') is inherently a whole-epic assertion and the schema has no multi-story AC ownership mechanism (see RETROSPECTIVE).
